_doc: Tinker Tray — the player's mental workbench. Select concepts, materials, recipes, or lore fragments to mull over. During rest/stability, progress ticks toward discoveries. Works for both crafting AND story/world secrets.

system.progressRate: per rest cycle, modified by settlement quality + relevant concept ranks
rules: Same tray = same result (deterministic, not random)
Not every combination yields something — dead ends teach you what doesn't connect
Partial progress hints: 'Something's forming but not quite there...'
Putting in a known recipe can yield a better version or reveal its underlying concepts
More concepts/materials known = more valid combinations available
Results require rest + stability (needs met, no danger) — not real-time gating

focus._doc: 5 slots available, player ranks them by priority. Top 2 get real focus. Items in slots 3-5 add noise and risk false connections. The player CAN fill all 5 but it's a trap — scattered thinking leads to wrong conclusions.

falseConclusions._doc: Wrong insights presented identically to real ones. Player cannot tell the difference until contradicted by evidence, NPC correction, or further discovery.
effects: Presented the same way as true insights — no UI indicator that it's wrong
Can temporarily block the correct discovery on that topic
Cleared by: contradicting evidence, NPC correction, or removing items and re-approaching with fewer
Some false conclusions are *partially* true — making them harder to discard
An NPC might say 'That's an interesting theory...' (neither confirming nor denying)

examples.falseResult: The dwarves dug too deep and released the corruption. It's their fault.
examples.truth: The corruption predates the mines. The dwarves stopped digging because they sensed it — they were cautious, not reckless.

examples.falseResult: The Wildkin are partially immune to corruption because of something in their diet.
examples.truth: They're not immune at all — they just recognize the early signs faster (animal instinct) and exile the affected before it spreads.

possibleResults.hint: The coiled metal wants to snap back...
The pivot point — if it were smoother...
Contained heat... what if it burst?
The mushroom doesn't burn — it makes other things burn faster
The grooves want to carry something... like channels for a river
Heat it, quench it, heat it again — the metal remembers
Every poison is a medicine at the wrong dose...
The corruption pulses... like a heartbeat. The Wildkin hear it before anyone.
The rune patterns on the ruins match the marks on construct shells exactly. Too exactly.
The dwarves stopped mining that vein for a reason. The metal down there... remembers.
The corrupted ones were clan-kin once. The Bear elders won't say it, but you can see it in how they fight them — with grief, not hatred.
The salt flats aren't natural. Someone — or something — salted the earth on purpose. To keep what's in the ocean from coming inland.
It doesn't spread like disease. It spreads like roots. The thing underground is growing.

progressModifiers.settlement-quality: Better compounds (garden, quarters, courtyard) = faster ticks
progressModifiers.relevant-concepts: Having higher ranks in related concepts speeds resolution
progressModifiers.npc-proximity: Resting near a relevant NPC (smith, enchanter, elder) can add a bonus
progressModifiers.location: Thinking about something while physically near a related place (ruins, mine, grove) helps
progressModifiers.repeated-dead-end: Trying the same dead-end combo multiple times eventually gives a clearer 'this leads nowhere' signal

loreFragmentSources: Talking to NPCs (especially elders, travelers, scholars)
Inspecting world objects (ruins, shrines, ancient machines)
Witnessing events (corruption spreading, beast transforming, ritual)
Reaching new areas for the first time

activePerception._doc: The Tinker Tray acts as a perception lens — what you're thinking about affects what you notice in the world. Active tray contents are checked against scene/NPC/dungeon tags to surface hidden content.
rules: Tray contents act as tag filters against world interactions
If overlap exists between tray and scene/NPC/dungeon tags, extra content surfaces
Extra dialogue options appear with relevant NPCs
Content is NOT permanently missable — return with the right tray later
But discovering in-the-moment gives bonus progress toward that discovery
Feels like player insight, not scripted — 'I noticed this because I was thinking about it'

examples.perception: The corruption pattern follows old clan boundary markers — it spread along the paths they walked
Extra dialogue: 'You notice the smith quenches in oil, not water. Ask why?' → teaches heat-treatment:2 faster
The marks on the wall aren't damage — they're machine-carved glyphs. You can read the pattern now.
Extra dialogue: 'The way the sails catch wind... the axle must absorb enormous force.' → accelerates bearings concept
Post-combat insight: 'The corruption concentrated in the bones, not the flesh. Bone stores things...'
